# Flask-RBAC

Adds RBAC support to Flask

## Installation

```
$ pip install flask-rbac
```

## Links

- [Document](https://flask-rbac.readthedocs.org)
- [Issue Track](https://github.com/shonenada/flask-rbac/issues)

## Contributes

You can send a pull request on
[GitHub](https://github.com/shonenada/flask-rbac/pulls).

classes.name: flask_rbac.model.RoleMixin

methods.name: flask_rbac.model.RoleMixin.get_name
documentation: Return the name of this role

methods.name: flask_rbac.model.RoleMixin.add_parent
documentation:
Add a parent to this role,
and add role itself to the parent's children set.
you should override this function if neccessary.

Example:

```
logged_user = RoleMixin('logged_user')
student = RoleMixin('student')
student.add_parent(logged_user)
```

```
param parent

Parent role to add in.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.model.RoleMixin.add_parents
documentation:
Add parents to this role. Also should override if neccessary.
Example:

```
editor_of_articles = RoleMixin('editor_of_articles')
editor_of_photonews = RoleMixin('editor_of_photonews')
editor_of_all = RoleMixin('editor_of_all')
editor_of_all.add_parents(editor_of_articles, editor_of_photonews)
```

```
param parents

Parents to add.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.model.RoleMixin.get_by_name
documentation:
A static method to return the role which has the input name.

```
param name

The name of role.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.model.RoleMixin.get_all
documentation: Return all existing roles

This provides implementations for the methods that Flask-RBAC wants
the role model to have.

```
param name

The name of role.
```

documentation:
Initialize application in Flask-RBAC.

Adds hook to authenticate permission before request.


methods.name: flask_rbac.RBAC.set_user_loader
documentation:
Set user loader, which is used to load current user.
An example:

```
from flask_login import current_user
rbac.set_user_loader(lambda: current_user)
```

```
param loader

Current user function.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.RBAC.set_hook
documentation:
Set hook which called when permission is denied
If you haven't set any hook, Flask-RBAC will call:

```
abort(403)
```

```
param hook

Hook function
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.RBAC.has_permission
documentation:
Return does the current user can access the resource.
Example:

```
@app.route('/some_url', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@rbac.allow(['anonymous'], ['GET'])
def a_view_func():
    return Response('Blah Blah...')
```

If you are not logged.

`rbac.has_permission('GET', 'a_view_func')` return True.
`rbac.has_permission('POST', 'a_view_func')` return False.

```
param method

The method wait to check.

param endpoint

The application endpoint.

param user

user who you need to check. Current user by default.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.RBAC.allow
documentation:
This is a decorator function.

You can allow roles to access the view func with it.

An example:

```
@app.route('/website/setting', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@rbac.allow(['administrator', 'super_user'], ['GET', 'POST'])
def website_setting():
    return Response('Setting page.')
```

```
param roles

List, each name of roles. Please note that,
 is refered to anonymous.
If you add  to the rule,
everyone can access the resource,
unless you deny other roles.

param methods

List, each name of methods.
methods is valid in ['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE']

param with_children

Whether allow children of roles as well.
True by default.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.RBAC.deny
documentation:
This is a decorator function.

You can deny roles to access the view func with it.

An example:

```
@app.route('/article/post', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@rbac.deny(['anonymous', 'unactivated_role'], ['GET', 'POST'])
def article_post():
    return Response('post page.')
```

```
param roles

List, each name of roles.

param methods

List, each name of methods.
methods is valid in ['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE']

param with_children

Whether allow children of roles as well.
True by default.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.RBAC.exempt
documentation:
Exempt a view function from being checked permission.
It is useful when you are using white list checking.

Example:

```
@app.route('/everyone/can/access')
@rbac.exempt
def everyone_can_access():
    return 'Hello~'
```

```
param view_func

The view function going to be exempted.
```

methods.name: flask_rbac.RBAC.get_app
documentation: Helper method that implements the logic to look up an application.

This class implements role-based access control module in Flask.
There are two way to initialize Flask-RBAC:

```
app = Flask(__name__)
rbac = RBAC(app)
```

or:

```
rbac = RBAC
def create_app():
    app = Flask(__name__)
    rbac.init_app(app)
    return app
```

```
param app

the Flask object

param role_model

custom role model

param user_model

custom user model

param user_loader

custom user loader, used to load current user

param permission_failed_hook

called when permission denied.
```
